title: Types of structure: functional, hierarchical (flat and narrow), matrix
summary: 9609 A Level — functional, tall/narrow, flat/wide, and matrix structures with pros, cons, and selection.
learningObjectives: Compare advantages and disadvantages of each.

simpleExplanation.title: Shapes of the organisation
simpleExplanation.summary: Functional groups by department; hierarchical adds layers of authority; flat reduces layers for speed; matrix combines functional and project lines.
steps: Tall narrow: many layers, tight control, slow communication.
Flat wide: few layers, wider span of control, faster decisions.
Matrix: staff report to functional and project managers.

flashcards.back: Departments by function — economies of scale, silo risk.
Many management levels — clear authority; slow, high cost.
Few layers, wide span — fast, cheap; managers overloaded.
Dual reporting — flexible projects; role conflict possible.
Subordinates per manager — wide span suits skilled staff.
Line of authority from top to bottom.
Functional — deep expertise in each department.

sections.content: **Types of structure** (7.1.2) — sketch **quick org charts** in exams. Label **span of control** and **chain of command** when discussing flat vs tall.

items: **Tall narrow:** Control and promotion paths; bureaucracy.
**Matrix:** Best for aerospace/consulting projects; expensive dual management.

sections.question: Engineering firm wins defence contract needing multidisciplinary teams (engineers, procurement, legal) for 18 months while maintaining functional departments. Recommend a structure.
sections.solution:
**Pure functional:** Departments slow to **integrate** for contract — **silo problem**.

**Pure project:** Loses **functional expertise** depth when contract ends.

**Matrix recommended:** Engineers report to **engineering head** and **project manager** — **flexible staffing** across contract life.

**Cost:** **Dual meetings**, **role ambiguity** — needs strong **7.2 communication** and **7.1.4 trust**.
